subtasks.description: Query vendor reliability data including vendor ID, name, incident count, average response time, uptime percentage, and other risk indicators

Classify each vendor into low_risk, medium_risk, or high_risk based on incident history and reliability metrics. Apply classification logic using thresholds on incident count, uptime, response time, and other risk signals to assign risk tiers

plan.cost_rationale: Two subtasks: (1) sql_gen (mid-tier) retrieves vendor reliability data from the warehouse with multi-table joins across vendor, incident, and SLA tables—requires mid-tier for reliable aggregation. (2) cross_recon (mid-tier) takes the SQL results and applies classification logic with multiple risk signals (incident history, uptime, response time) to assign risk tiers—mid-tier because the classification requires multi-signal reasoning and threshold application. No separate policy_action needed; the classification is part of the reconciliation output. This is the minimum decomposition: a single data fetch followed by a single classification step.

output.sql: SELECT vendor_name AS vendor_id, vendor_name, COUNT(CASE WHEN status NOT IN ('paid', 'cleared') THEN 1 END) AS incident_count, ROUND(AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (paid_date - invoice_date)))::NUMERIC / 86400, 2) AS average_response_time_days, ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN status = 'paid' THEN 1 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 2) AS uptime_percentage, CASE WHEN COUNT(CASE WHEN status NOT IN ('paid', 'cleared') THEN 1 END) > 5 THEN 'high_risk' WHEN COUNT(CASE WHEN status NOT IN ('paid', 'cleared') THEN 1 END) > 2 THEN 'medium_risk' ELSE 'low_risk' END AS risk_classification FROM invoice_facts WHERE invoice_date >= '2024-01-01' GROUP BY vendor_name ORDER BY incident_count DESC, average_response_time_days DESC
